title: Plesilia cuneifolia
taxonomy: Plesilia cuneifolia belongs to the family Lamiaceae, which includes many species of mint, basil, and oregano. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics, such as its square stem and oppositely arranged leaves. The genus Plesilia is part of a larger group within the Lamiaceae family, which is often referred to as the "lip-shaped" clade. Further classification places Plesilia cuneifolia as a species within the subfamily Lamioideae. The specific classification of Plesilia cuneifolia is subject to ongoing research and potential revision.
